page: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the arm64 architecture, specifically the AWS Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 192 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem demonstrates exceptional bandwidth, achieving top-tier results in both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. Additionally, the server excels in database operations, static web serving throughput, and multi-threaded decompression tasks, all ranking in the top 10%. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, falls into the strong to average categories.

Due to the lack of local storage and dedicated GPUs, this instance relies on network-attached storage and is not suited for hardware-accelerated graphics or deep learning training. However, its high core density and memory bandwidth make it highly cost-efficient for scale-out compute workloads, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. It is highly recommended for multi-threaded workloads such as database operations, CPU-based LLM inference, and high-performance computing tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated physical arm64 cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. Equipped with 192 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory, it provides a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but features a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high throughput for database operations and static web serving. Conversely, single-core execution and certain text-generation tasks perform in the strong to average ranges. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for compute-intensive, multi-threaded workloads, including software compilation, database management, and high-throughput web serving, offering a cost-efficient profile for dense scale-out deployments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 96 dedicated arm64 cores, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 dedicated arm64 cores and 192 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-throughput compute workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized arm64 server featuring ninety-six dedicated physical cores and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 arm64 physical cores at 2.8 GHz.
Equipped with 192 GB of high-bandwidth DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core).
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance.
Features 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs.
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
